item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Placard Name: The Crying Boy
Description: A canvas is set up in the room. A sheet is draped over it.

Placard Name: Living Nightmare
Description: A dirty twin mattress on a simple metal frame rests in the middle of the room. A figure is lying on the mattress with a sheet pulled up over them.

Placard Name: Mr. Silence
Description: A tall, black, wooden box rests against the back wall. It is bound in chains and locks. A bright purple "W" is emblazoned on its front in gold trim.

Fucking chills, man. I wish I could convey images this well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite wonderful, the mystery of this article is very good despite how short it is, +1

A lot of people say this is disappointing because of a lack of explanation or finale. I think I would agree *if* this article were slowly ramping up or teasing a grand reveal only not to deliver, but in my opinion what you see is what you get: it's some gritty, grimey, skeezy version of a containment facility, courtesy of the D of A. The concept alone is enough without further twists.

item.title: DoA Clipboard Question
item.content:encoded: Since there was a clipboard added that lists other Department of Abnormalities, what are the requirements in order for a DoA article to be added to that list? Other than it being about the Department of Abnormalities of course. I'm mainly asking since there are more than 3 DoA articles than what's on that list besides SCP-3790 (and partially because there was originally 4 DoA articles on that list).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thoroughly enjoyed this, the atmosphere is fantastic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Noting that "Adam's hatred" on floor three likely is a reference to TWIE as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone in this article is just fantastic. I remember reading this for the first time a while back and being completely immersed into it. The minimalist diction and atmosphere still manages to creep me out every time I read it again.

Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: Hesitant -1
item.content:encoded:
I really want to upvote this, but there are just too many things that don't make sense to me.

+ show block
– hide block

"SCP Foundation Department of Abnormalities" Isn't this redundant? The foundation already deals with abnormalities, granted, they are just called skips or anomalies, but the latter is a synonym. Is this implying that the Foundation wasn't originally dedicated to anomalies?

So the O5s locked it. That just feels like kind of a cop out. It makes the article artificially deeper, making it seem like its steeped in mystery, making the reader grasp at whatever details they can to make connections. In-universe, O5s locked the door, and have a single guard. That's it? A dedicated group could get right in there. The security doesn't make sense. If they did know about it and want to hide something down there, why was it just left there? Security by obscurity, having it in a random location, is not a good security measure. Hell, an abandoned warehouse? I'm surprised we didn't run into any homeless people or junkies in there in the Exploration Logs.

The abnormalities down there…aren't anything special. We have dozens of skips in containment that pose threats to reality itself, but these guys need to be kept super top secret?

Unless this whole thing is an example of SCP-2747 infection, because there are 6 layers, but we never get to see the seventh. I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this might be my favorite piece on the site so far. I forgot to upvote it when I first read it, but I've fixed that mistake. +1

For a more immersive reading experience, I totally recommend listening to the "Empty Bliss Beyond This World" album by The Caretaker while reading, it makes it feel so much more eery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I translated it because I personally like this work, I'm not really sure why tho, my feelings for this are complicated. I really love when article has something deeper hidden in it. And DoA is one of my favourites when it comes to minor GoI things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately I gotta agree with many previous comments. It’s creepy, it’s weird, it’s cool to decipher the references, but on the whole it’s not enough to just throw twenty potentially spooky, unconnected ideas without any overarching narrative or explanation. Because it just abruptly ends and is no longer intriguing, just frustrating and confusing.
Congrats. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think “hello” is SCP-3935. That’s an arc word right there.

item.title: Explanation of the seventh floor
item.content:encoded:
Spoilarz

+ show block
– hide block

3790 was envisioned as something akin to Roget's Proposal, that is, a facility capable of containing anything thrown at it. Something powerful was used in its making. So they threw many things at it. SCP-3935, an anomalous The Crying Boy, whatever the heck Mr. Silence is, possibly SCP-2740, and many others. But then, a research lab within discovered something.

The anafabula.

That’s why there have been no investigations into the seventh floor. Any such would be erased. This may be what happened to the Department of Abnormalities. They tangled with the anafabula and their ideal containment building held it back. However, it decreased Foundation influence from what it once was. Additionally, the building still holds the antinarrativic space, and all who enter floor seven are consumed.


+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sheer dread of this SCP is just perfect. I had trouble sleeping after first reading this

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of it being a precursor comes from the less scientific categorization and management of anomalies, but you're right, it's not known and there is evidence against this view.

The name "Department of Abnormalities" suggests that it is either a secret department within the Foundation, an organization that already deals with the abnormal, or that it was from an earlier time when different terminology was used.

The important aspect is that it's not known, no record remains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it possible the supposedly big-shot abnormalities and the mysteriously swept-under-the-rug Level Seven are intentionally there to draw our attention away from the thing being hidden?

Perhaps from the empty Room Three on Level One or the hallway behind "Room" 4 on Level Two?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a Minecraft map based on this, and Kaktus seemed to think it was good, so here's a video of it: https://youtu.be/UGZ_Og9J1hY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CK-Class reality shifts sure are a bitch, aren't they?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where do people get the idea that the DoA is somekind of precursor to the modern Foundation? It was shut down after the invention of SCP-804, has a TV in it, and is labeled like a sub-department of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ignore double post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An explanation I like for this skip is the idea that initially, the SCP Foundation was a much more multifaceted organization than the one we know today, with the containment of the anomalous (or the "abnormal") only being a subcategory of its larger mission stagement. What the SCP Foundation's original focus actually was, I don't know, but what matters is that at some point, the Department of Abnormalities performed a coup d'état, using the anomalies under their care to rewrite history and establish themselves as the primary — and only — branch of the Foundation. I'm not sure what their reasons for doing this would be, but in any case, such an explanation would shed quite a bit of doubt on the O5s and their true motives. This would explain, at the very least, why the majority of the document is restricted to O5 access, and no attempts to explore the 7th floor have been made; the O5s know damn well what's down there, and they don't want anyone else to catch wind of it and start piecing things together.

Or maybe I'm just a sucker for the idea that the Foundation as we know it is nothing more than a masquerade that was only recently lied into existence and this explanation doesn't actually fit with the other depictions of the DoA currently on the site. Either way, this is a deeply unsettling SCP, and it captures exactly the kind of horror I love.
Well deserving of the rating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was reading this at 3AM, you madlad.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm calling it; floor 7 has something related to 2747 on it; it's something relating to the goal-that-can't-be-reached part of the narrative

item.title: Objects Abnormalities
item.content:encoded:
Gunna try my best at explaining the objects powers the best I can. A quick forward, if these are accurate, these objects could all have varying power levels. As such it makes me wonder about how their power might be amplified for the "Failed End of the World" idea, and I've posted my ideas for the most powerful versions below. These do make sense with the "Scrapped ideas" theory though.

My best guesses
Probably mostly wrong

1.1, Vivaldi: The violin of Vivaldi, a famous Italian composer, his most famous work being Four Seasons. It could be that this one is Neutralized with the broken bow, but before that it could have been able to control the seasons, which in turn might destroy the world due to the weather phenomenon.

1.2, Montezuma's Face: Lot's of possibilities here, one of them being Montezuma's Curse, a disease in central america which causes people to get horrible bowel bashing diarrhea. The ticking adds the idea of there being a countdown before the box is opened. It could be a reference to the Aztec Calendar, where the box would open, the world would be destroyed by everyone getting diarrhea. Neutralized by time and failure to activate.

1.3, Empty: The Chamber is Empty. There is NOTHING inside of it. This could be in reference to a certain tale: The Stars Do Not Wait For You, where the embodiment of nothingness consumed the world, and everything they tried to stop it failed.

1.4, Bones: This one seems odd. The scratches mean something was trying to get out, and the bones mean something died, or broke off part of itself (i.e. cut off an arm) to escape. Now. Does anyone know something the foundation keeps with claws that could easily damage itself in order to escape containment so it could go kill things elsewhere? Something that perhaps doesn't mind loosing a limb, or might even regrow it?

2.1, Ian: Thin pale man in a straitjacket and blindfold. I kinda feel like this is a reference to something but I don't know what it is. That being said, there are only so many scps that have a straitjacket, mainly the ones that would hurt themselves otherwise, as ones that would hurt others usually get MORE restraint. The blindfold makes me believe its something that can look at you and do something. Honestly, this one, I just don't know.

2.2, The Crying Boy: Oh! This one's easy. Google the Crying Boy, it's a creepy painting said to be cursed to cause whatever area its in to burn down. This adds to the theory that this area is resistant to abnormal effects, since it hasn't burned yet. So how could it end the world? Put it outside, and watch as the world burns.

2.3, The Watchers: Hmm, well, it's clear they're watching (or trying to watch) something in that area we can't see. They're only vaguely humanoid, and there's three of them, leading me to believe they're a reference to the Mario Hell-Valley Sky Trees, an asset in Super Mario Galaxy that stands in the background of one level and watches you ominously. Dunno how you could make this end the world other than by making everyone paranoid.

2.4, Hallway: Best guess, it's another Recurring Hallway. There's another SCP where you go through one door and end up back in the same hallway, the further you go, the weirder it becomes due to you traveling into another universe. Chances are this hallway leads onto infinity, or another universe. Could be something there that wants us dead? Hard to tell really. Could also be a reference to 087.

3.1, The Infinite Cold: Yeah, everyone pretty much agrees, this is 3799.

3.2, Sorrow: Four parallel lines, and the object taken makes me believe this might be a recording of the song Gloomy Sunday a song so sad as to convince people to commit suicide. Bit of a stretch, but with everything else seeming to be a reference it makes sense, plus the four lines could also be an allusion to musical strands, which a single verse of traditionally has four parts separated by lines.

3.3, World without man: SCP 804. How it escaped, or why it's not there, we will never know. On the theory these are scrapped ideas, please remember, djkaktus is doing some really damn good rewrites of older articles.

3.4, Adam's Hatred: Adam seems to be a really powerful reality bender. This might be something he created or spawned that has his level of reality bending that could, if let out, destroy the world.

4.1, The Morning Star: Another name for Satan true, but it also seems to be implied to be the sword guarding the gates of Eden, as it's said to burn with intense flames. (Possibly scrapped due to SCP-001) Could destroy the world as implied in SCP-001

4.2, Wormwood: Biblical reference to a bright star that falls to earth and is a big part of Armageddon. (SCP-1591?)

4.3, Harmonia's Necklace: A necklace of the goddess Harmonia, that is said to curse whoever owns it with terrible misfortune. Not sure what the hole and candle are for though.

4.4, Darkness: Paranoia? Darkness? What goes bump in the night? Unsure, could be based off of the SCP (I don't remember and can't find it) where the Foundation retroactively create fear in the mind of all humans to prevent the end of the world from some aliens in the dark. Before the fear was created making you look over your shoulder, these things could have easily conquered the fearless humans. Fear is poison to them.

5.1, Heart of Man: Reference to GTA IV's Heart of the City Easter Egg, where a heart in chains is kept in the Statue of Liberty analogue. This one probably has the power to kill all humans if it stops beating.

5.2, Hello: Someone said it's SCP-3935. I'm willing to believe that and leave it there. It ends the world if the madness grows.

5.3, Channel 55: There's a creepypasta (really a crap tone of them) about a certain tv channel that either kills you, someone else, drives you to madness, ect. I think one of them is 55? I can't seem to find it though, and the Creepypasta wiki is not helping. It could also be a reference to horror movies… Someone said SCP-055 reference, but I don't really see that since we can, well, remember it.

5.4, Living Nightmare: 231's Sister. One of them giving birth could be an XK scenario. The sheet pulled over them implies they're dead/Neutralized, and their horrors are already out and about, (or if you believe the theory this area is anomaly proof, they're still IN her).

6.1, Mr. Silence: Doc makes toys, they're meant to be fun, but one step removed from safety and reality makes them too dangerous. Redd is said to be why he stopped. What if this came before Redd? The Mrs all seem (at least to me) to be either pure toys (Fish, Soap, Hot), or used to contain something Wondertainment (who is implied to be God) wants under locks more than the foundation. (Smile Scary, being the Factory, Redd, the Scarlet King, and Brass, Mekhane). What could he be containing so much that he chained it in a coffin given to the foundation?

6.2, Dead Man's Chair: Easy one! There's an Urban legend about a chair that a condemned man sat in before being captured by the police. On death row he cursed the chair so that anyone else who sits in it will die. And the curse seems to hold true. How could this end the world? What if the shadow spreads to other chairs via shadows. What if it gets to SCP-3311? That's far fetched, sure, but still…

6.3, Otzi: THE ICE MAN COMETH! Erm, it seems like a guy with the powers of SCP-2082. That power plus intelligence and learning capabilities could be very bad indeed.

6.4, Appolyon's Crown: SCP-2317's crown. Probably not literal, but it probably DOES contain whatever will break the last chain. (Also, nice in joke there kaktus, Appolyon = Abadon = The Great Devourer, Aka what 2317 is called in SCP-001, The Scarlet King)

7: I'm guessing it's djKaktus' second proposal. Probably wrong though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how the descriptions give the image of anomalies that have been reduced to something almost… sad. The Infinite Cold melted. It seems like nothing got to Apollyon's Crown. Some are just 'gone'. Although they're anomalies, they've been covered up and hidden away, and lost their abject horror. Or the descriptions are all lies/misdirection/understatement as part of a cover-up. OR whatever's kept people from being curious enough to open the doors or try to access the seventh floor has also changed the perception of the anomalies. Any way, I think this one's got the good kind of open-ended vagueness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I see the issue with this one not having a conclusion or ending that feels fitting or satisfying, I enjoyed the oddness of this one. There's enough open here that many readers can and will pull their own canon on this.

Did the world ending failures all get locked down here? Is this where they failed? Did they "succeed" in ending the world but in this contained site, locked away on their own pocket dimensions? Is this a real Foundation station? Is this a parody of the Foundation, hacked into the site via some anomalous group to jest?

My coffee is kicking in and I'm missing most of the references here but good luck when I take one of these ideas and run with it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat, huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
spoilers for TWIE
(spoiler for twie)

oh hey, it's the sword that Aaron retreived

It's probably my own ADHD hyperfixation, but I've been having a LOT of fun trying to map out how all the djkaktus tales link with each other and with other tales - even if it doesn't actually mean anything in the end. It's somehow satisfying to see in real(-ish) time how an author works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After a third read, definite upvote. From the first, the intentionally unsettling atmosphere was really effective for me, but it was this read where something clicked. This reads like almost an inverse 1730 (I was actually surprised seeing this was a Kaktus article). Instead of the expectations set up with this sort of anomaly, the Foundation doesn't send in D-Class personnel, MTFs, even a drone. It lends this feeling that, firstly, this was something they gave the bare minimum of exploration into, possibly relating to the O5 lock? Secondly, it creates an almost genuine feeling, at least to me, of doing exactly what the in-universe authors of the document are doing: looking through small windows at unexplained, unsettling objects that we don't understand. That's the strongest point of the atmosphere here: anything that'd be recognizably human, or even alive, is alienated from us, kinda dehumanized/put in the uncanny valley. There's nothing in there we can easily identify or put a face to, and that's what really makes is disturbing.

item.title: The remainder of this file has been locked by order of the Overseer Council.
item.content:encoded: Gee guess I better go read something else

item.title: I just realized
item.content:encoded:
"The watchers" could be referencing SCP-1806.
That one mentions shadowed beings called "Watchers" squating in the shadows

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like a shotgun fire of one off spooky images. Those images certainly are spooky. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's definitely a reference, intentional or otherwise. The fact that we don't know what's down there on the 7th level is probably meant to inspire wonder and paranoia, which is key to SCP-2747. The only thing different here is the punchline. Whatever is down there is not supposed to be accessed, and going to the 7th floor anyway is apparently a bad idea.

Whatever's on level 7 would help us understand why the facility is no longer used, but that's not for us to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People have been talking about the "seven floors, with one inaccessible" being a reference to the Scarlet King, but could it also possibly be alluding to SCP-2747? One of the recurring themes of the nonexistent works of fiction in SCP-2747 are groups of seven things, one of which is missing, inaccessible, or otherwise obscured in some way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this sort of skip. There are a lot of moments that can be considered fridge horror, such as: Level 4 Room 4, Level 6 Room 4, and the fact that people are unable go to the 7th floor for whatever reason. Also, something that I thought of to make this SCP possibly have more horror is to hint at an existence of an 8th floor.

Room Three
Placard Name: Channel 55
Description: A small, CRT television sits in the center of the room. Something is playing on the screen, but a dark cloth has been draped over the top of it.

When I first read that, I thought it was gonna reference SCP-055

item.title: Hmmmmmmm
item.content:encoded:
So, this is interesting.

What is here is clearly the aftermath of some cover-up. The "Locked by O5 Council" thing, the missing Foundation records, the apparent uncharacteristic lack of any attempt to further study the rooms or to enter the seventh floor… Someone in high places wants to keep whatever happened here, or whatever is contained here quiet.

What sets this apart from the numerous other similarly themed skips is that this is actually a cover-up done right. No clever rebels sneaking info and links into the article, no ostentatious blackboxes that have contents easily inferred. There actually isn't enough info to figure out what is here, unless there's something I'm missing.

Definitely a plus for theme and realism (the Foundation is for once competent at hiding information). But does it work overall as an article? I don't know. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that would rather ruin the suspense, wouldn't it? Some things are better left veiled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. Maybe there should be an addendum where the researchers find a way onto floor 7 and discover hints to the existence of the department?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I know what this thing is, it’s a housing chamber for scps that are considered some of the most dangerous of them all. Ones that need to be looked up and undisturbed so as to prevent them from ever posing s threat, that’s probably why the foundation has no knowledge of ever having an abnormalities department, because they erased all evidence of it and it’s contents…or at least tried to, so that what the things inside would stay buried forever.
I think I know what some of these objects are, more or less
1. The infinite cold: the some of the melted remains of scp-3790, god know what it would do in liquid form
2.vivaldi: Vivaldi was an Italian musical composer and violinist, this is probably his violin. Scp-012 was found in an Italian tomb. What happens if you play scp-012 on vivaldi’s Violin…
3. Mr silence: obviously a wondertainment creation and part of the “little misters” line. The question is what is he,. Judging by his name and the chains on his comtainment I don’t think we would want to find out
4.hello: hello
5. A world without man: duh
6. Apollyons crown: his crown
7. Adams hatred: throughout various scps adam(from Adam and Eve) is said to have become some kind of reality-bending monster, could this have something to do with him
8. The heart of man: the literal heart of man? It that if it were to stop beating all of human would face death

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Room One
Placard Name: Vivaldi
Description: Chamber is empty aside from a violin propped in the far corner of the room. The bow is broken on the ground in front of it.

The first thing that came to my mind was buff Vivaldi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, no. I agree with DrakkenZero. It just feels more like the Foundation is being lazy on this one, and I feel like that could use some elaboration in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's also Wormwood (4000 entry), Mr. Silence (Wondertainment) and Ötzi, although I'm not sure how that last one fits in. I would wish for another layer in this article, one that shows what exactly is being obscured here. Right now, to me, this is only one half of an article.

item.title: Hey, I like this a lot.
item.content:encoded:
I was bored at work and read a bunch of 3000 series SCPs. Long articles with 6 explorations logs, format screws that probably require a 4-year degree to make, the whole genre of "pataphysics. But this SCP, this one right here, is the one I'm going to be thinking about when I go to sleep tonight.

Look at those containment procedures! No reality anchors, no cognitohazards, no "cannot be contained" blurbs. Just a padlock, a guard, and a big "do not enter" stamp from the O5 council. There isn't even any exploration logs or testing files. Yet with 6/7 floors, 4 entries per floor, you managed to establish an incredibly dense atmosphere with just enough strands to grasp onto and draw the beginnings of a theory off of. I love it.
Congrats! I see a lot of potential from the Department of Abnormalities and I'm excited to see what else is in store for it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably because of somebody powerful and spooky told them not to touch it.

Is there anything in the article itself to suggest that though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 2317 reference followed closely by the seventh level being impossible to access makes me wonder if there’s a connection to the Scarlet King.

The recent 001 proposal about what the SK is, being an entity that rides the line between modernity and the primeval, makes me wonder if there’s something to it. Rambling ahead.

This is either something anomalously ripped out of or into reality, or some sort of Foundation blacksite so black that not even O5 know about its existence. A lot of the things contained here are references to other SCPs. Could it be that this place is some kind of barometer for the SCP Foundation’s total containment of the anomalous? The 001’s take on the SK states that the more the Foundation tries to analyze it, the more it grows in power and rage. If everything in the facility is known, then all seven seals are broken?

I dunno. The more I ponder this SCP the more I like it. I started out as a novote but I’m upvoting after writing all that. The only other SCP I’ve pondered so much after reading is the anafabula, and that’s high fucking praise for this entire site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reminds me of the gallery in the JRPG "Ib", which while I don't exactly "love"it, I don't really hate it either. This made me ask questions: what if SCP hadn't always been "Special Containment Procedure"? What if our motto hadn't always been "Secure, Contain, Protect", but it became such along the way?
+1 spook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, Series 1 is "here's everything we know, it's not much but we tried." The foundation has a fistfull of threads with this site that they just aren't following. Probably because of somebody powerful and spooky told them not to touch it.

Which is valid, but definitely isn't in the spirit of series 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wouldn't surprise me if the things here are almost every scrapped idea you've ever had, and whatever's on the final floor is an idea that you don't want anyone else to know about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s fair too. I think my problem is this is on the edge of being really mysterious in a good way and being so mysterious that no story is actually being told. Idk, this is probably a personal preference thing or I’m missing something

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers, maybe.

+ show block
– hide block

The Infinite Cold

IIRC kaktus had a Series 3 that was eventually self-deleted titled "Awake in The Infinite Cold". It was a grate under a lake that lead to a lakeside house - through the window of which creepy shit could be observed.

This, and his earlier comment here:

Something that got buried a long time that should never see the light of day again.

Has me wondering if these aren't all scrapped ideas, which, if they are, this is a pretty damn impressive way to breath new life into them.

canaryfarmer points out on p.3 of the discussion here, that the name could be shortened to "DOA", further solidifying this in my headcanon.

Doesn't exactly jive with everything (World Without Man, for example), but who knows?

Eta: this was supposed to be a reply to Malice's post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I very much liked that the area's name can be shortened to 'SCP Foundation DOA'.

- ominous music plays suddenly -

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reads like the setting of an old-school creepypasta (probably called 'The Facility' or something). The imagery has charm, and is structured well, but I couldn't shake the feeling that this is throwing a bunch of stuff at the wall and seeing what sticks, and that it ended up with maybe 1.5-2x as many entries as it needed, or else that better things could easily have replaced a bunch of the entries (you use almost every version of the 'we don't know what is/was in this chamber' card imaginable). Also not a big fan of the World Without man Crosslink, since it seems like the reference works at cross purposes with SCP-804's actual story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay. I love this stuff. This stuff is 100% my jam. Kaktus, Croque, you've outdone yourselves once again.

+ Never fear, boys and girls
- Detective Malice is on the case!

I'm going to try to work out what's going on here, given all the references to other stuff.

Placard Name: Montezuma's Face
Description: An ornate wooden chest with gold inlays and a large steel lock sits on a table in the middle of the chamber. A quiet clicking sound, like that of a clock, is barely audible from within the chamber.

Okay, so I think this is a reference to SCP-902, The Final Countdown. A brief google search tells me "Montezuma's Face" is some kind of legend about the ancient Aztec king awakening after a long sleep to restore his kingdom to glory, so that might have something to do with it.

Placard Name: Ian
Description: A thin, pale man in a straitjacket wearing a blindfold sits in the corner of the chamber.

I immediately thought 096, but Ian suggests not. The only skip (I know of) with a connection to the name Ian is SCP-2306, but I can't really see the connection there other than them both being trapped.

Placard Name: The Infinite Cold
Description: The floor of the chamber is covered with a thin layer of water. The interior of the room seems to be considerably larger than its exterior physical dimensions would allow.

I immediately thought SCP-3799. Given that it's water instead of ice it suggests that, somehow, it's… melted? They either neutralised or contained it for so long that it melted into water? Unless it's some other "Infinite Cold", like the Matchbox Full of Winter, or just a new, unknown anomaly. Weird stuff.

Placard Name: World Without Man
Description: Chamber is empty.

Definitely SCP-804, unless it's intentionally misleading. I don't know why there's nothing in it, but I can think of two plausible explanations: 1) it somehow self-neutralised and destroyed itself, or 2) it was removed from the chamber, which suggests the current 804 was contained here. Also, it implies this structure wasn't built by man, or is somehow super-resistant to anomalous effects.

Placard Name: Tool marks indicate that placard has been pried off. The word "hello" is scratched into the metal where the placard should be.
Description: Sliding panel is welded shut.

This terrifies me. I'm not sure why. A welded panel (to stop something being seen) and a cryptic message on the outside of the chamber? Definitely an abnormality.

Placard Name: Mr. Silence
Description: A tall, black, wooden box rests against the back wall. It is bound in chains and locks. A bright purple "W" is emblazoned on its front in gold trim.

Wondertainment!

Placard Name: Ötzi
Description: A layer of ice covers the viewport and obscures visibility. A dark shape can be seen in the middle of the room, although no details can be distinguished.

Ötzi, as anyone who spends enough time wandering through wikipedia will know, is Europe's oldest mummy. He's also known as the Iceman.

So, this place looks like it's containing anomalies. Or rather, abnormalities. Are they abnormalities by our standards or, and this is my new headcanon, abnormalities by the Foundation's standards. Is this a place where the Foundation locks up things that are too strange even for them? If so, what could be so strange that they'd have to seal up a whole level to contain it? Or is this something mimicking the Foundation, and if so why the sealed-up level?

God, I love all of this so much. I do get the feeling I might be imposing meaning where there is none, though, so if anybody else has noticed anything else about it let me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it works quite well as an atmospheric piece. Instantly got the hair standing up on the back of my neck, which is always a good sign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always complain about how you end up explaining far too much sometimes, and this is, in my humble opinion, a nice change. I actually felt completely creeped out by the old good series 1 chills. That alone deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, I think we benefit enormously from articles which don't end by tying everything up into a neat little narrative bow.

That's what makes series 1 so compelling: Almost every article you read leaves you with more questions than answers. That's a neat trick, and something I definitely support moving toward.
